verdict_reasoning: PSCA is explicitly described as a glycosylphosphatidylinositol (GPI)-anchored cell membrane glycoprotein. GPI anchors covalently attach proteins to the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane via a stable phosphatidylinositol moiety, providing robust surface accessibility. The LY6/PLAUR domain-family membership is consistent with canonical GPI-anchored surface antigens. Multi-tissue expression (prostate, bladder, placenta, colon, kidney, stomach) and up-regulation in prostate cancer confirm its surface phenotype.
